title: dactylorhiza fuchsii var. brachystachys
intro: Dactylorhiza fuchsii var. brachystachys, also known as the short-spurred marsh orchid, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the common spotted orchid (Dactylorhiza fuchsii), a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which are thought to help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza fuchsii var. brachystachys include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and even lower blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested in the summer months, dried, and then used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties for various health benefits.
